rulings.Case Title: PAUL BOSSERT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Cooper Industries, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production and, even if it had, there is a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant based on plaintiff¿s deposition and the documents attached to defendant¿s General Order interrogatory responses. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Wilson¿s declaration are sustained. Summary adjudication is granted as to Issue 1 (false representation) and Issue 2 (punitive damages). Plaintiff did not oppose these issues. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DIANE HALL et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant AmeriGas Propane, L.P.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HARMON GUY HIGGINS JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ric-Wil, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Ric-Wil, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant¿s alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first (negligence) and second (products liability) causes of action. A triable issue is created by plaintiff¿s discovery responses and deposition testimony, defendant¿s General Order interrogatory responses and the deposition of Mr. Zeile, defendant¿s person most knowledgeable, whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the third (false representation), eighteenth (concert of action), and nineteenth and twentieth (fraud, deceit/negligent misrepresentation/concealment) causes of action and as to punitive damages. Plaintiff did not oppose these issues. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration, plaintiff¿s deposition, defendant¿s General Order interrogatory responses, the deposition of Mr. Zeile and plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ANTRIM VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Swinerton Builder'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Swinerton Builder's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant¿s alternative motion for summary adjudication Issue 1 (premises owner/contractor liability) is granted in part and denied in part. It is granted as to defendant¿s retained control and hazardous condition arguments. It is denied as to the allegedly negligent actions of defendant¿s employees working in the presence of plaintiff. Defendant¿s motion for summary adjudication as to Issue 2 (punitive damages) is off calendar based on plaintiff¿s representation that this claim is being withdrawn, although there is not yet a dismissal on file. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ANTRIM VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Anning-Johnson Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Anning-Johnson Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and discovery responses, Mr. Warren¿s deposition testimony, and Mr. Ay¿s declaration and its attachments create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s alternative motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as to the claims for strict products liability and false representation because those claims have been dismissed. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration and the documents attached to it, are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ANTRIM VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Praxair Distribution, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Praxair Distribution, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (asbestos blankets) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. =(220/HEK)
On calendar for Wednesday, August 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed on August 12. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BEULAH MAE SIGSBEY et al VS. A.O. SMITH CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Carrier Corp.¿s demurrer to all claims alleged against is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiffs¿ failure to allege a specific product attributable to defendant renders the complaint deficient under Bockrath. For guidance of the parties in the future but without making a ruling on these issues, the fifth cause of action does not appear to be sufficient to withstand a pleading challenge, while the other causes of action challenged by defendant appear to be sufficient if the Bockrath issue is resolved. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BEULAH MAE SIGSBEY et al VS. A.O. SMITH CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Carrier Corporation¿s motion to strike punitive damages is moot due to the tentative ruling on the demurrer. For guidance of the plaintiffs in the future but without making a ruling on this issue, the allegations of the complaint regarding punitive damages do not appear to be sufficient to withstand a pleading challenge. =(220/HEK)
